title: Colly Collectors, Callback Order, Limits, Storage, and Adoption Boundaries · Failure Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in policy-preserving Colly adapter and decision record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Map Collector creation, callback registration, Visit, Request, Async, Wait, error, and close-related ownership into the application lifecycle.
Translate allowed domains, depth, body size, redirects, robots, rate limits, revisit, and user agent from owned policy and verify gaps.
Keep callbacks thin, order-independent where required, concurrency-safe, context-rich, and delegated to testable application services.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Colly Lifecycle
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 1973 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to map Colly lifecycle, policy, callbacks, storage, and differentials without losing owned invariants; every URL, body, tree, record, frontier transition, and report row retains causal identity; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Map Collector creation, callback registration, Visit, Request, Async, Wait, error, and close-related ownership into the application lifecycle.

Inspect the Colly Lifecycle trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Colly Lifecycle.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map collector creation, callback registration, visit, request, async, wait, error, and close-related ownership into the application lifecycle. For Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Colly Lifecycle, keep Colly Lifecycle connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

For inspect the failure record, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Framework Differential
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-framework-differential-xnjwbx" that uses case 9398 to map Colly lifecycle, policy, callbacks, storage, and differentials without losing owned invariants. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Framework Differential.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Framework Differential into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Framework Differential, keep Framework Differential connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
